args.code:
import json
from datetime import datetime

# Get the full query result from the support database
support_cases = [
    {"id": "500Wt00000DE00gIAD", "createddate": "2020-09-29T01:59:00.000+0000", "orderitemid__c": "802Wt00000796bfIAA", "subject": "SecureAnalytics Compliance Concern", "status": "Waiting on Customer"},
    {"id": "500Wt00000DDy8bIAD", "createddate": "2020-11-05T08:19:00.000+0000", "orderitemid__c": "802Wt00000796qFIAQ", "subject": "Update Alerts Absent", "status": "Closed"},
    {"id": "500Wt00000DE0NFIA1", "createddate": "2020-11-09T19:02:00.000+0000", "orderitemid__c": "802Wt00000796qFIAQ", "subject": "Missing Feature Notifications", "status": "Closed"},
    {"id": "500Wt00000DE0AMIA1", "createddate": "2021-03-07T11:25:00.000+0000", "orderitemid__c": "802Wt00000796qFIAQ", "subject": "No Feature Update Notices", "status": "Closed"}
]

# Define the date range (past 10 months from 2021-04-10)
end_date = datetime(2021, 4, 10)
start_date = datetime(2020, 6, 10)  # 10 months prior

print(f"Date range: {start_date.date()} to {end_date.date()}")

# Filter cases within the date range and count by month
month_counts = {}
for case in support_cases:
    created_date_str = case['createddate']
    if created_date_str != 'None' and 'T' in created_date_str:
        created_date = datetime.strptime(created_date_str.split('T')[0], '%Y-%m-%d')
        
        # Check if date is within range
        if start_date <= created_date <= end_date:
            month_key = created_date.strftime('%Y-%m')
            month_name = created_date.strftime('%B %Y')
            
            if month_key not in month_counts:
                month_counts[month_key] = {'name': month_name, 'count': 0}
            
            month_counts[month_key]['count'] += 1
            print(f"Found case: {case['id']} on {created_date.date()} - {case['subject']}")

# Sort by count descending
sorted_months = sorted(month_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]['count'], reverse=True)

print("\nCases by month:")
for month_key, data in sorted_months:
    print(f"  {data['name']}: {data['count']} cases")

# Get the month with most cases
if sorted_months:
    top_month = sorted_months[0]
    print(f"\nMonth with most cases: {top_month[1]['name']} ({top_month[1]['count']} cases)")
    
    result = top_month[1]['name'].split()[0]  # Just the month name
else:
    result = "No cases found"

print(f'__RESULT__:')
print(result)

import json
from datetime import datetime

# Get the full query result from the support database
support_cases = [
    {"id": "500Wt00000DE00gIAD", "createddate": "2020-09-29T01:59:00.000+0000", "orderitemid__c": "802Wt00000796bfIAA", "subject": "SecureAnalytics Compliance Concern", "status": "Waiting on Customer"},
    {"id": "500Wt00000DDy8bIAD", "createddate": "2020-11-05T08:19:00.000+0000", "orderitemid__c": "802Wt00000796qFIAQ", "subject": "Update Alerts Absent", "status": "Closed"},
    {"id": "500Wt00000DE0NFIA1", "createddate": "2020-11-09T19:02:00.000+0000", "orderitemid__c": "802Wt00000796qFIAQ", "subject": "Missing Feature Notifications", "status": "Closed"},
    {"id": "500Wt00000DE0AMIA1", "createddate": "2021-03-07T11:25:00.000+0000", "orderitemid__c": "802Wt00000796qFIAQ", "subject": "No Feature Update Notices", "status": "Closed"}
]

# Define the date range (past 10 months from 2021-04-10)
end_date = datetime(2021, 4, 10)
start_date = datetime(2020, 6, 10)  # 10 months prior

print(f"Date range: {start_date.date()} to {end_date.date()}")

# Filter cases within the date range and count by month
month_counts = {}
for case in support_cases:
    created_date_str = case['createddate']
    if created_date_str != 'None' and 'T' in created_date_str:
        created_date = datetime.strptime(created_date_str.split('T')[0], '%Y-%m-%d')
        
        # Check if date is within range
        if start_date <= created_date <= end_date:
            month_key = created_date.strftime('%Y-%m')
            month_name = created_date.strftime('%B %Y')
            
            if month_key not in month_counts:
                month_counts[month_key] = {'name': month_name, 'count': 0}
            
            month_counts[month_key]['count'] += 1
            print(f"Found case: {case['id']} on {created_date.date()} - {case['subject']}")

# Sort by count descending
sorted_months = sorted(month_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]['count'], reverse=True)

print("\nCases by month:")
for month_key, data in sorted_months:
    print(f"  {data['name']}: {data['count']} cases")

# Get the month with most cases
if sorted_months:
    top_month = sorted_months[0]
    print(f"\nMonth with most cases: {top_month[1]['name']} ({top_month[1]['count']} cases)")
    
    result = top_month[1]['name'].split()[0]  # Just the month name
else:
    result = "No cases found"

print(f'__RESULT__:')
print(result)

var_functions.query_db:5.subject: Scalability Issue
var_functions.query_db:5.description: I am facing challenges in scaling the OptiPower Manager to meet my organization's growing demands, hindering our expansion efforts.

args.code:
import json

# The cases data from the support database
cases = [
    {"id": "500Wt00000DE00gIAD", "createddate": "2020-09-29T01:59:00.000+0000", "orderitemid__c": "802Wt00000796bfIAA", "subject": "SecureAnalytics Compliance Concern", "status": "Waiting on Customer"},
    {"id": "500Wt00000DDy8bIAD", "createddate": "2020-11-05T08:19:00.000+0000", "orderitemid__c": "802Wt00000796qFIAQ", "subject": "Update Alerts Absent", "status": "Closed"},
    {"id": "500Wt00000DE0NFIA1", "createddate": "2020-11-09T19:02:00.000+0000", "orderitemid__c": "802Wt00000796qFIAQ", "subject": "Missing Feature Notifications", "status": "Closed"},
    {"id": "500Wt00000DE0AMIA1", "createddate": "2021-03-07T11:25:00.000+0000", "orderitemid__c": "802Wt00000796qFIAQ", "subject": "No Feature Update Notices", "status": "Closed"}
]

# Count cases by month
month_counts = {}
for case in cases:
    date_str = case["createddate"]
    # Extract month and year
    if "T" in date_str:
        date_part = date_str.split("T")[0]
        year_month = date_part[:7]  # YYYY-MM
        month_name = date_part[5:7]  # MM
        
        if year_month not in month_counts:
            month_counts[year_month] = 0
        month_counts[year_month] += 1

# Get the month with most cases
max_month = max(month_counts.keys(), key=lambda k: month_counts[k])
month_names = {
    "06": "June",
    "07": "July", 
    "08": "August",
    "09": "September",
    "10": "October",
    "11": "November",
    "12": "December",
    "01": "January",
    "02": "February",
    "03": "March",
    "04": "April"
}

result = month_names.get(max_month.split("-")[1], "Unknown")
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

# Get the month with most cases
